custom_theme_settings.value: Shankar Chowk, Ward No. 6, PO + Dist — Madhubani, Bihar — 847211, India
Ward No. 09, Mohalla Tharesari, Behind Meena Bazaar Chowk, Near R.K. College Gate No. 2, Madhubani — 847213, Bihar
Protecting children and building rural livelihoods across Northern Bihar.
Join Sarvo Prayas Sansthan in protecting children from abuse, marriage and trafficking, and building sustainable livelihoods for rural families across Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul.
Making communities a better place to live in.

posts.html:
Our Story Sarvo Prayas Sansthan (SPS) was started in 2004 by a self-motivated group of women to work in Northern Bihar, in the spirit of voluntarism and social development. SPS chose to operate in the Madhubani and Darbhanga districts, a complex, diverse and risk-prone region considered among the most backward in the country, popularly known as the flood-prone region of Bihar.

This part of rural India had long remained neglected by the state and by mainstream development agencies. SPS was born to take on exactly these challenges, working directly with the most marginalised communities.

Our Mission To empower the poor anuj and sushant, disadvantaged and marginalised, especially women and children, by protecting their rights, improving livelihood opportunities, and building a just and equal society where every person can live with dignity and security.

Our Vision We believe in community development through participatory approaches, making communities a better place to live in, where children are safe in school and families earn their livelihoods with confidence.

Where We Work Our work is concentrated in the flood-prone districts of Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul, some of the most underserved regions of rural India. We work block by block, village by village, building local committees that carry the work forward themselves.
posts.plaintext:
Our Story Sarvo Prayas Sansthan (SPS) was started in 2004 by a self-motivated group of women to work in Northern Bihar, in the spirit of voluntarism and social development. SPS chose to operate in the Madhubani and Darbhanga districts, a complex, diverse and risk-prone region considered among the most backward in the country, popularly known as the flood-prone region of Bihar.

This part of rural India had long remained neglected by the state and by mainstream development agencies. SPS was born to take on exactly these challenges, working directly with the most marginalised communities.

Our Mission To empower the poor anuj and sushant, disadvantaged and marginalised, especially women and children, by protecting their rights, improving livelihood opportunities, and building a just and equal society where every person can live with dignity and security.

Our Vision We believe in community development through participatory approaches, making communities a better place to live in, where children are safe in school and families earn their livelihoods with confidence.

Where We Work Our work is concentrated in the flood-prone districts of Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul, some of the most underserved regions of rural India. We work block by block, village by village, building local committees that carry the work forward themselves.
posts.custom_excerpt: From a small group of determined women in 2004 to a trusted voice for child protection and rural livelihoods across Northern Bihar.

posts.title: Our Milestones
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"2004 ","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Founded by a self-motivated group of women in Madhubani; registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"listitem","version":1,"value":1},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"2006–07 ","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Obtained 12A and 80G registrations; launched first major child protection and health programmes.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"listitem","version":1,"value":2},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"2022 onwards ","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Through Just Rights for Children, prevented 75+ child marriages across Madhubani and Supaul.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"listitem","version":1,"value":3},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Present ","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Active across Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul  reaching 4,000+ families.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"listitem","version":1,"value":4}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"list","version":1,"listType":"bullet","start":1,"tag":"ul"}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
posts.html:
• 2004 Founded by a self-motivated group of women in Madhubani; registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.
  • 2006–07 Obtained 12A and 80G registrations; launched first major child protection and health programmes.
  • 2022 onwards Through Just Rights for Children, prevented 75+ child marriages across Madhubani and Supaul.
  • Present Active across Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul reaching 4,000+ families.
posts.plaintext:
* 2004 Founded by a self-motivated group of women in Madhubani; registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.
 * 2006–07 Obtained 12A and 80G registrations; launched first major child protection and health programmes.
 * 2022 onwards Through Just Rights for Children, prevented 75+ child marriages across Madhubani and Supaul.
 * Present Active across Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul reaching 4,000+ families.

posts.html:
Sarvo Prayas Sansthan (SPS) was started in 2004 by a self-motivated group of women to work in Northern Bihar, in the spirit of voluntarism and social development. SPS chose to operate in the Madhubani and Darbhanga districts, a complex, diverse and risk-prone region considered among the most backward in the country, popularly known as the flood-prone region of Bihar.

This part of rural India had long remained neglected by the state and by mainstream development agencies. Even after decades of independence, scattered efforts had made little real difference to the quality of life and livelihoods of its people. SPS was born to take on exactly these challenges, working directly with the most marginalised communities.

SPS was born to take on exactly these challenges, working directly with the most marginalised communities.

posts.title: Rooted in Northern Bihar
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Our work is concentrated in the flood prone districts of ","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Madhubani, Darbhanga ","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"and","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":" Supaul","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":", some of the most underserved regions of rural India. We work block by block, village by village, building local committees and groups that can carry the work forward themselves.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Our core strategy rests on three pillars: ","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"capacity building, coordination and convergence","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":", identifying with the communities we serve, and helping those same communities identify with us.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
posts.html:
Our work is concentrated in the flood prone districts of Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul, some of the most underserved regions of rural India. We work block by block, village by village, building local committees and groups that can carry the work forward themselves.

Our core strategy rests on three pillars: capacity building, coordination and convergence, identifying with the communities we serve, and helping those same communities identify with us.
posts.plaintext:
Our work is concentrated in the flood prone districts of Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul, some of the most underserved regions of rural India. We work block by block, village by village, building local committees and groups that can carry the work forward themselves.

Our core strategy rests on three pillars: capacity building, coordination and convergence, identifying with the communities we serve, and helping those same communities identify with us.

posts.title: Child Protection
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"We work to protect children from child marriage, child labour, trafficking and abuse, and to bring them back into school, through Village Child Protection Committees and adolescent groups that resolve issues from within the community itself.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}

posts.title: Livelihood

posts.title: Two causes. One community.

posts.custom_excerpt: For over two decades SPS has tackled two deeply connected challenges holding Bihar's rural families back.

posts.title: Started by women, for the community.
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Founded in 2004 by a group of determined women in Madhubani, Sarvo Prayas Sansthan began with a simple conviction: the most marginalised communities in flood-prone Northern Bihar deserved focused support, not tokenism.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Today SPS works across Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul districts with partners including LEHER, Heifer International India, Just Rights for Children, Goonj, and several government schemes.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
posts.html:
Founded in 2004 by a group of determined women in Madhubani, Sarvo Prayas Sansthan began with a simple conviction: the most marginalised communities in flood-prone Northern Bihar deserved focused support, not tokenism.

Today SPS works across Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul districts with partners including LEHER, Heifer International India, Just Rights for Children, Goonj, and several government schemes.
posts.plaintext:
Founded in 2004 by a group of determined women in Madhubani, Sarvo Prayas Sansthan began with a simple conviction: the most marginalised communities in flood-prone Northern Bihar deserved focused support, not tokenism.

Today SPS works across Madhubani, Darbhanga and Supaul districts with partners including LEHER, Heifer International India, Just Rights for Children, Goonj, and several government schemes.
